_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — TIER BREAK from Tier 1 (Levy) to Tier 2 (Conscript Sergeant). Take the L3 'Veteran Levy' composited keeper and produce the L4 'Conscript Sergeant' variant.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1_sword/L3/composite/infantry_sword_L3_v1_composited.png (L3 keeper denoised + composited against L2 keeper)
_meta.input_image_anchor: UnitProgression/Infantry/Refs/L1_Sword_Base.png (1024x1024 native — scale and framing reference only, content not applied)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Infantry/Infantry_Progression_Plan.md (L4 — Conscript Sergeant, TIER BREAK) (SWORD VARIANT — see Infantry_Sword_Progression.md)
history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L3_to_L4.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Sword-specific weapon beats applied at L3, L4, L5, L6, L7, L10 (see WEAPON_BEAT_OVERRIDES in build_sword_prompts.py for the per-level deltas).
_meta.tier: Tier 2 — Conscript Sergeant (TIER BREAK from Tier 1) — SWORD VARIANT
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2 — composite-only pipeline locked for Infantry): two inputs = L3 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor. Tier-break composite step uses --dilate 15.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L3 'Veteran Levy' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every L2 + L3 feature exactly as shown in the first input image: (a) QUILTED BROWN LEATHER UNDER-TUNIC SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms (matte warm-brown leather, diagonal cross-stitching) — these stay UNDER the new L4 scale-mail sleeves; (b) WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle centered at front; (c) SMALL BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT side); (d) BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with cream bone-handle knife on the anatomical-LEFT hip (viewer-RIGHT side, opposite the pouch); (e) SINGLE STEEL PAULDRON CAP on the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder (sword-arm, viewer-LEFT) — the new L4 second pauldron arrives on the OTHER shoulder, this existing one stays where it is; (f) BRASS QUILLON CAPS at the cross-guard tips (small warm-brass bulb caps at each end of the cross-guard, distinctly warm brass tone against the cool steel cross-guard) — these MUST REMAIN at this level; (g) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS radiating from the central steel boss in a cross pattern on the wooden shield face. DO NOT revert any of these features. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, marking the TIER BREAK from Tier 1 Levy to Tier 2 Conscript Sergeant. The new kit introduces metal at the SLEEVES (not yet on the chest) and brings the second shoulder + the shield rim into a fully metalled silhouette while keeping the red+mustard split livery tunic visibly DOMINANT on the chest. (1) LONGER CROSS-GUARD WITH DOWNWARD-CURLED QUILLON TIPS PLUS DEEPENED BLADE FULLER — extend the existing cross-guard from approximately 4 inches wide to approximately 5 inches wide, with the outer tips of each quillon now subtly curved DOWNWARD toward the blade (a gentle inward sweep at the tips, not a full curl). The L3 brass quillon caps remain at the new tips. ALSO deepen the central fuller on the blade — the shallow vertical groove running down the center of the blade is now more pronounced, approximately twice as deep and slightly wider, giving the blade a clearer center-line shadow. The blade material remains cool silver-gray polished steel; the cross-guard remains cool silver-gray steel with the brass tip caps.  (2) SCALE-MAIL SHORT SLEEVES ON BOTH UPPER ARMS — add chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel covering both upper arms from the shoulder down to mid-bicep, sitting OVER the existing L2 quilted leather short sleeves. Each scale is a small rounded shield-shape (semi-circular at the top, slightly tapered at the bottom), arranged in horizontal rows where each row overlaps the row below by half a scale. The texture reads as chunky, hand-painted, clearly individual scales — NOT chainmail rings, NOT fine ring mesh, NOT fish-scale fine texture, NOT a quilted diamond pattern. The scale-mail sleeves match the existing mail coif's scale style exactly. The scale sleeves end at mid-bicep; the L2 quilted leather under-tunic sleeve is still briefly visible as a narrow ~1-inch band of warm-brown leather between the lower edge of the scale-mail sleeve and the upper edge of each leather forearm bracer. (3) CONTINUOUS IRON RIM BAND AROUND THE SHIELD PERIMETER — add a continuous dark cool-gray iron rim band wrapping the entire perimeter of the round shield, approximately half an inch wide, sitting OUTSIDE the existing ring of L2 rim rivets (the rivets are now contained within or just inside the new rim band). The L3 four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss remain visible against the wooden plank face between the central steel boss and the new continuous iron rim. The wooden plank face of the shield is still visible between the cross arms and the rim. (4) SECOND STEEL PAULDRON ON THE SHIELD-ARM SHOULDER — add a steel pauldron cap on the anatomical-LEFT shoulder (shield-arm side, viewer-RIGHT) matching the existing L3 pauldron on the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder. Both pauldrons are now slightly LARGER than the L3 single cap (approximately 5 inches in diameter, up from 4 inches at L3) — the silhouette mightiness rule applies: the shoulder envelope should read visibly broader than at L3. The pauldrons are cool silver-gray polished steel, slight domed curve, with three or four visible dark-iron rivets along each pauldron's lower seam. Each pauldron rivets to a thin warm-brown leather strap mostly hidden under the livery tunic. (5) STEEL CHEST-STRAP RIVET PLATE — add a small rounded steel plate centered on the chest where the leather pauldron-supporting straps cross over the livery tunic. The plate is approximately 3 inches in diameter, cool silver-gray steel, with four corner dark-iron rivets visible. The plate sits in the upper chest area roughly centered between the two pauldrons, riveted onto a thin leather X-strap that crosses the chest under the plate. The livery tunic chest panels remain fully visible AROUND the plate; the plate is small and does NOT cover the red+mustard split. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks reaching upper neck — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild, NOT long; his dark mustache continuous with the beard; his dark-brown short hair as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering scalp, ears, and neck down to collarbone; the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT half solid crimson-red, viewer-RIGHT half mustard-yellow with a vertical crimson-red center stripe — this red+mustard split chest livery remains visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible, NOT covered by scale mail (scale mail is at the SLEEVES only at L4, NOT on the chest), NOT shrunk into a thin strip; the existing brown leather forearm bracers wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms; the existing quilted leather short sleeves under the new scale-mail sleeves (briefly visible at the bicep gap); the existing wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle; the existing brown leather hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT); the existing brown leather knife sheath with cream bone-handle knife on the anatomical-LEFT hip (viewer-RIGHT); the existing leather grip wrapping the entire grip of the sword. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, hand grips the sword's leather grip just below the sword blade, sword blade extending downward and toward viewer-LEFT with the blade tip near hip height. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body bringing the round shield up to upper-chest / shoulder height with the shield's flat face presenting toward the viewer at slight three-quarter angle. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced, NOT a swing, NOT a charge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the SECOND input image exactly — L1_Base scale anchor) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall) and approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide, including the sword and shield). TOP of mail coif at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at approximately 16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. Approximately 18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, 20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT revert any L2 or L3 feature: DO NOT remove the quilted leather sleeves; DO NOT replace the leather belt with a rope cord; DO NOT remove the hip pouch, knife sheath, sword grip grip cord, or shield cross-bands; DO NOT remove the existing single steel pauldron on the sword-arm shoulder (the L4 second pauldron arrives on the OTHER shoulder); DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the red+mustard split livery tunic — it MUST remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible BETWEEN the two pauldrons and AROUND the new chest-strap rivet plate; DO NOT cover the tunic chest with scale mail, chain mail, breastplate, or any body armor — scale mail at L4 is at the SLEEVES ONLY, NOT on the chest; DO NOT extend scale mail to the upper chest, lower chest, or replace the tunic; DO NOT extend scale mail downward into a long skirt past the tunic hem — that arrives at L5; DO NOT replace the sword blade with a flanged steel head — the wood + spikes + new deepened central blade fuller configuration is the L4 sword; DO NOT change the cross-guard shape on the sword blade; DO NOT add iron banding to the sword grip (sword grip reinforcement strip arrives at L6); DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, square shield, or any non-round shape; DO NOT replace the wooden shield face with metal — that arrives at L5 (steel-faced); DO NOT add an embossed sun-burst boss or sun-burst boss pattern — those arrive at L7; DO NOT add a steel breastplate, cuirass, plackart, or any chest plate — those arrive at L5+; DO NOT add a bevor / steel collar / steel throat plate — those arrive at L5; DO NOT add a helm, sallet, kettle helm, or any structural addition to the head — head covering remains the existing mail coif; DO NOT add steel vambraces or replace the leather forearm bracers — those arrive at L5; DO NOT add steel sabatons, greaves, leg armor, tassets, fauld, or any leg / hip armor at this level (sabatons arrive at L6; tassets at L6); DO NOT make the pauldrons layered multi-disc — at L4 each pauldron is a single rounded steel cap (slightly larger than L3); layered 2-disc pauldrons arrive at L6; DO NOT add a second sword-arm pauldron piece — exactly ONE steel cap per shoulder at L4; DO NOT make the chest-strap rivet plate larger than ~3 inches; DO NOT extend the chest-strap rivet plate into a full breastplate; DO NOT make the L4 scale-mail sleeves into chainmail rings or fine ring mesh — they MUST be chunky overlapping rounded scale plates matching the mail coif scale style; DO NOT make the deepened central fuller on the sword blade into smooth tubes — they are flat iron banding strips wrapping the wood; DO NOT make the iron rim band on the shield wider than half an inch; DO NOT add gold trim, gold rivets, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at L8; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or vertical helm extensions (no helm at L4); DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT change the hair, the hood/coif color, the livery colors, the trouser color, or the boot color; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white — the background must be clean opaque white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
